discovery.run_summary: Sharp risk-off day driven by semiconductor wreckage — SMH down ~7% on what appears to be a chip-sector pivot trade flagged in CNBC. Counter-rotation into defensives (XLP, XLV, XLU green) and small banks (KRE +1.6%). Iran/Hormuz de-escalation news is pushing oil softer despite XLE holding green; VIX +13% reflects the tech-led drawdown rather than broad panic.

notable_index_moves: SMH -7% with NVDA, TSM, AVGO, ORCL all down 3-7% — semis taking the brunt
Defensive rotation clear: XLP +1.87%, XLV +1.41%, XLU +0.78%, XLRE +1.41%
VIX +12.79% to 19.49 — elevated but not panic; concentrated in tech
KRE +1.57% suggests regional banks benefiting from rotation, not credit fear

discoveries.name: Acuity Inc.
discoveries.setup: Pre-earnings de-risking the day before a pre-market print
discoveries.thesis: AYI down ~7% on 1.5x volume with earnings tomorrow morning — this is positioning/de-risking ahead of the print rather than a fundamental tell. Industrials (XLI -2%) are weak but AYI's drop is roughly 3x the sector, suggesting nervous holders trimming into the catalyst. If the print is in-line or better, a sharp snap-back is likely given the magnitude of pre-positioning; if guidance is cut, today's drop will look prescient. The asymmetry tilts modestly bullish into the print because the expectation bar has been visibly lowered.
discoveries.what_confirms: AYI earnings tomorrow meet/beat with maintained FY guide; commentary on lighting/building-management demand stable; no destocking commentary
discoveries.what_kills: Pre-announcement tonight, channel-check leaks suggesting verticals miss, or print shows margin compression from tariff pass-through failures
discoveries.what_to_learn: Mid-cap industrials dropping 5%+ on above-average volume within 48 hours of earnings, with no 8-K and no peer-specific news, are usually positioning trades — the magnitude of pre-print de-risking is itself a sentiment signal worth tracking against the actual print.
discoveries.catalyst: Earnings due 2026-06-25 pre-market; broad industrials weakness adding to the move
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals shows upcoming_earnings in 1.6 days pre-market; no 8-K filed; volume 1.54x average is elevated but not extreme — consistent with positioning rather than a leak
research_pointers: Check AYI's last 4 quarters' pre-print 1-day moves vs post-print reactions to gauge whether pre-positioning typically reverses
Pull sell-side estimate revision trend over past 30 days — has the bar been lowered already?
Lighting/building automation peer commentary (HUBB, NVT) for read-throughs on end-market demand

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.thesis: Onto down ~9% with no company-specific catalyst — empty catalyst_signals, volume exactly at average. SMH -7%, TSM -6.7%, NVDA -4%, LSCC -7%, AEIS -6% all moving same direction. ONTO as semi-equipment exposure gets dragged proportionally. The magnitude is roughly justified by the sector move; not obviously over- or underdone. Flagging as RATIONAL for audit trail — this is a beta event, not a mispricing.
discoveries.what_confirms: SMH continues lower tomorrow with ONTO tracking; no idiosyncratic news emerges
discoveries.what_kills: ONTO-specific bad news (customer concentration disclosure, order cancellation) surfaces that would re-rate the move as fundamental rather than sympathy
discoveries.catalyst: Broad semi-equipment selloff; CNBC flagged traders setting up cheap downside bets against chip stocks after record highs
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + volume at exactly 1.0x average + entire semi complex down sharply = sector beta, not stock-specific news
research_pointers: If ONTO sells off further on similar SMH magnitude tomorrow, the asymmetry may shift to UNDERDONE-fade given lithography/inspection exposure to advanced nodes
Check whether ONTO's revenue mix to memory vs logic customers makes today's drag justified relative to TSM-specific weakness

discoveries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
discoveries.setup: Continued multi-day drawdown in coking coal name with Reg FD filing recently
discoveries.thesis: AMR down nearly 5% today and ~12% over 5 days, with a 7.01 Reg FD disclosure filed last week. Reg FD items are catch-all — could be investor presentation, could be guidance update — and without the filing contents we cannot tell which. Coking coal pricing is also pressured by global steel demand uncertainty. Cannot construct a 3+ confidence call in either direction without reading the 6/18 8-K exhibit.
discoveries.what_confirms: Reading the 6/18 8-K exhibit reveals downward met-coal guide or contract pricing reset — would confirm OVERDONE-on-deeper-look or UNDERDONE-with-more-to-come depending on content
discoveries.what_kills: Filing turns out to be benign (conference presentation) — then today's selling is pure commodity tape and likely RATIONAL given met-coal spot weakness
discoveries.what_to_learn: Reg FD 8-Ks (item 7.01) without visible exhibit content are dead-ends for catalyst analysis until the actual press release/deck is read — flag for manual review rather than directional call.
discoveries.catalyst: 6/18 Reg FD 8-K of unclear content; ongoing met-coal commodity weakness
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K items 7.01 + 9.01 with no visible details; 5-day drawdown larger than today's move suggests known overhang from the filing
research_pointers: Pull the 6/18 8-K exhibit directly — is it a guide cut, presentation, or contract update?
Track Australian premium hard coking coal spot prices over the same 5-day window for sector context
Check whether other met-coal names (METC, HCC, WHC) moved similarly — if so, commodity story; if not, AMR-specific

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.setup: Small-cap selling on elevated volume with no catalyst, in a name with prior pre-earnings positioning patterns
discoveries.thesis: CALX -3% today on 2.66x average volume — the volume spike is the interesting signal, not the price move. No 8-K, no scheduled earnings, no obvious peer drag (broadband-access equipment isn't part of the SMH wreckage). High-volume selling in a $2.3B name without identifiable catalyst often reflects fund-level repositioning that reverses when the seller is done. Tech-sector beta weak today provides cover but doesn't fully explain. Modest fade setup if next session shows volume normalizing.
discoveries.what_confirms: Volume normalizes on a bounce attempt tomorrow; no negative news surfaces from a customer (BSP rural broadband funding cuts would be the obvious risk)
discoveries.what_kills: BEAD/rural broadband funding cut announcement or major tier-1 telco capex revision — this would justify and extend the move
discoveries.what_to_learn: Volume multiple >2.5x on a sub-5% price move in a small/mid-cap with empty catalyst_signals is often a single-fund unwind — worth watching the next 1-2 sessions for either a sharp reversal or news that explains the seller.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — elevated volume without visible news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals + 2.66x volume + price move modest relative to the volume spike
research_pointers: Check for any 13D/13G or fund-position changes in CALX over past 48 hours
Monitor BEAD program funding news given CALX's exposure to rural broadband buildouts
If volume reverses on a bounce, confirms forced-selling thesis

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Semiconductor sector pivot — SMH -7% after recent record highs, with options market showing traders buying cheap downside
Iran/Hormuz de-escalation — Trump confirms Strait stays open, sanctions relief into escrow, record oil throughput

secondary_opportunities.relationship: sympathy — energy commodities pressured by easing geopolitical premium

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump confirms Strait of Hormuz remains open, no naval blockade, sanctions relief into U.S.-controlled escrow for Iranian purchases of U.S. corn/wheat/soybeans/medical supplies
trump_signals.note: Bearish for crude/energy-tape risk premium (XLE held up today only because broad tape sold off harder elsewhere). Modest tailwind for U.S. ag majors via escrow-funded purchases — worth checking ADM/BG/CORN for any pre-positioning. Coking coal not directly affected but commodities-complex sentiment turns softer with geopolitical premium draining.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump claims record 19M barrels through Hormuz yesterday, oil prices 'tumbling'
trump_signals.note: Confirms direction of de-escalation narrative; reinforces fade in energy risk premium that began with the ceasefire framework. Watch whether E&Ps with Permian/domestic exposure (less geo-premium sensitive) outperform internationals.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic's Claude Tag brings always-on AI teammate to Slack, capturing org context
ai_announcements.summary: Claude Tag embeds into Slack to learn institutional knowledge and workflows. Strategic play on enterprise context layer. Competitive implications for Slack/Salesforce-native AI (Slack AI, Einstein), Microsoft Copilot in Teams, Glean, and standalone enterprise knowledge tools.
ai_announcements._note: None of the moving stocks in today's market_data have a clear, direct read-through to Claude Tag. The selloffs in ONTO, LSCC, AEIS (semi-cap/semis), AYI (lighting/pre-earnings), BMI (water meters), CALX (broadband), BEAM (biotech), AMR (coking coal) are not mechanistically linked to a Slack-embedded AI assistant. Forcing a thesis here would be manufactured signal.

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI launches initiative to find and patch open source bugs using AI
ai_announcements.summary: AI-assisted vulnerability discovery and remediation across open source. Read-through to cybersecurity names (CRWD, S, PANW, FTNT, SNYK-private) is mixed: more vulns surfaced = more remediation spend (bullish counter-thesis), but also commoditizes some SAST/code-scanning vendors.
ai_announcements._note: No cybersecurity tickers in today's mover list, so no actionable call.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's biggest movers (ONTO -9.2%, LSCC -6.9%, AEIS -6.0%, AYI -7.1%, BMI -5.2%, CALX -3.0%, BEAM -6.6%, AMR -4.8%) do not map cleanly to the AI announcements in the news feed. ONTO/LSCC/AEIS look like a semi-equipment/analog group selloff (possible sector rotation or a peer guide-down — needs a non-AI catalyst check, not in this dataset). AYI is 1.6 days from earnings — move is earnings-positioning, not AI-driven. BEAM is biotech, unrelated. AMR is coking coal, unrelated. Honest read: quiet AI-news night relative to today's tape; the selloffs are likely non-AI catalysts Michael should source elsewhere. Confidence: 4.
